description_long: This is a well-worn, cylindrical fire extinguisher with a faded orange-red body, showing significant scuffing, scratches, and dirt, indicating heavy use or long-term exposure in an urban environment. The top features a dark, metallic cap with a prominent handle and a thick, flexible black hose attached, which is secured to the body with a metal bracket. The front and side surfaces display white instructional labels and pictograms, some of which are partially worn or obscured by grime. The hose is long, rubberized, and slightly bent, with a metallic nozzle at the end, and is held in place by a bracket when not in use. The extinguisher's base is flat, allowing it to stand upright, and the overall construction appears robust, likely made of steel with rubber and plastic components. The utilitarian design and visible Cyrillic text suggest it is intended for public or industrial use in a Russian-speaking region.
description: A heavily used, orange-red fire extinguisher with a black hose, metal handle, and worn white instructional labels. The body is scratched and dirty, with a robust, utilitarian design.
description_view_0: The front view shows the orange-red body, black cap, and handle, with the hose draped over the top and secured to the side.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the white instructional labels and pictograms, as well as the full length of the black hose and its metallic nozzle.
description_view_2: The opposite side view displays more worn labels and the attachment point of the hose bracket, with the handle and cap visible.
description_view_3: The rear view shows the handle, cap, and the hose bracket, with the hose hanging down and the body mostly free of labels.
attribute_label_or_signage: White instructional labels and pictograms with Cyrillic text are present on the front and side surfaces, rectangular in shape and partially worn.
attribute_mounting_bracket_or_holder: A metal bracket is attached to the side of the body, securing the hose in place.
attribute_hose_or_nozzle: A thick, black rubber hose is attached to the top, approximately the length of the extinguisher body, with a metallic nozzle at the end, secured by a bracket.
